content_markdown: The temperature of an ideal gas is a measure of the molecules' average

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram present in the image.

vibe_explanation: According to the kinetic theory of gases, the absolute temperature $T$ of an ideal gas is directly proportional to the average random translational kinetic energy of its molecules, expressed by the relation $\bar{E}_k = \frac{3}{2}k_B T$.
